target.name: DiamFab
target.founder: Khaled Driche, Gauthier Chicot, Ivan Llaurado

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
DiamFab is a seed-stage company in the Chips & Semiconductors sector that has raised €8.7M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 total comparables: 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

The Peer-Median method is not applicable here. The sector contains 10 same-sector peers, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints in any single category. A median derived from the full all-sector peer set would lack sufficient homogeneity to be reliable for DiamFab's specific profile and would risk obscuring material differences in technology maturity, capital efficiency, and addressable market positioning within semiconductors.

Given the constraint on narrow peer triangulation, this analysis cannot establish a defensible valuation range on the basis of comparable company multiples alone. Further work is needed: either expanding the dataset to include additional same-stage or adjacent-stage semiconductor firms, or applying revenue multiple or DCF-based methods if forward projections and margin assumptions can be validated. The key uncertainty remains whether DiamFab's technology and go-to-market profile align more closely with specialized chip designers (lower revenue scales) or foundry-adjacent businesses (higher capex and customer concentration risk).
